pr.title: Rotate session tokens on privilege change
pr.author: alice
pr.body:
Following the security review last quarter, we need to invalidate existing session tokens whenever a user's role or scope changes. This PR adds a `rotate()` hook on the session store and wires it into the role-update flow.

Closes #138.

hunks.body:
def get(self, token: str) -> Session | None:
         row = self._db.fetchone("SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE token = ?", (token,))
         return Session.from_row(row) if row else None
 
-    def create(self, user_id: int) -> Session:
-        token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
-        self._db.execute(
-            "INSERT INTO sessions (token, user_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
-            (token, user_id, time.time()),
-        )
-        return Session(token=token, user_id=user_id)
+    def create(self, user_id: int, scope: str = "default") -> Session:
+        token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
+        now = time.time()
+        self._db.execute(
+            "INSERT INTO sessions (token, user_id, scope, created_at, rotated_at) "
+            "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
+            (token, user_id, scope, now, now),
+        )
+        return Session(token=token, user_id=user_id, scope=scope)
+
+    def rotate(self, user_id: int) -> int:
+        """Invalidate all live sessions for a user. Returns count rotated."""
+        cur = self._db.execute(
+            "DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id = ?", (user_id,)
+        )
+        return cur.rowcount
+
+    def touch(self, token: str) -> None:
+        self._db.execute(
+            "UPDATE sessions SET rotated_at = ? WHERE token = ?",
+            (time.time(), token),
+        )

chunks.summary: SessionStore gains rotate() and touch(); create() takes scope and a rotated_at timestamp
chunks.rationale_for_position: Core mechanism — every other change in this PR is a caller of these new methods, so we anchor the mental model here first

conn.execute(
         "UPDATE users SET role = ? WHERE id = ?", (new_role, user_id)
     )
+    # Privilege change invalidates existing tokens (PR #142).
+    rotated = session_store.rotate(user_id)
+    log.info("rotated %d sessions for user %d", rotated, user_id)
     conn.commit()

chunks.summary: update_role() now calls session_store.rotate() before commit
chunks.rationale_for_position: The first caller of the new API. The ordering of UPDATE/rotate/commit is the security-meaningful part

hunks.body:
+import time
+
+import pytest
+
+from auth.session import SessionStore
+
+
+@pytest.fixture
+def store(tmp_db):
+    return SessionStore(tmp_db)
+
+
+def test_create_session_assigns_token(store):
+    s = store.create(user_id=7)
+    assert s.token
+    assert store.get(s.token) is not None
+
+
+def test_rotate_invalidates_all_user_sessions(store):
+    a = store.create(user_id=7)
+    b = store.create(user_id=7)
+    c = store.create(user_id=8)
+
+    rotated = store.rotate(user_id=7)
+
+    assert rotated == 2
+    assert store.get(a.token) is None
+    assert store.get(b.token) is None
+    assert store.get(c.token) is not None
+
+
+def test_rotate_returns_zero_when_no_sessions(store):
+    assert store.rotate(user_id=999) == 0
+
+
+def test_touch_updates_rotated_at(store):
+    s = store.create(user_id=7)
+    time.sleep(0.01)
+    store.touch(s.token)

chunks.summary: New test file covering rotate(), create() scope, and touch()
chunks.rationale_for_position: Tests last — once the production behavior is understood, the tests confirm what we expect (and let us spot missing coverage)
